title: Aspalathus kougaensis
taxonomy: Aspalathus kougaensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a member of the genus Aspalathus, which includes several species of shrubs found in South Africa. The species is further grouped under the subfamily Faboideae, which consists of legumes with similar characteristics. Aspalathus kougaensis is specifically categorized as a part of the subgenus Aspalathus, which comprises plants that are typically shrub-like in appearance. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Aspalathus kougaensis from other related species.

intro: Aspalathus kougaensis, commonly known as Kouga buchu, is a shrubby plant native to South Africa, belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from digestive issues, such as diarrhea and bloating, as well as acting as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. The bioactive constituents of Kouga buchu include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Kouga buchu include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat gastrointestinal problems and promote overall well-being.
